slides.title: [Client name] detailed review
slides.subtitle: [Scope, audience, timing, and decision lens for the standard diligence deck]

slides.title: [One detailed diligence claim]
slides.strapline: [Frame the issue with the evidence base, why it matters to the deal, and what leadership should do next.]
body: [Summarize the current state with a specific operating or financial fact, then explain why that fact is material to the diligence conclusion.]
[Describe the most important pressure point with evidence from management, process review, or data analysis so the point feels grounded and defendable.]
[Explain the operational or financial implication in the language of sequencing, control, margin, or transaction value rather than generic concern.]
[Set out the recommended action with the owner, timing, and first milestone so the slide points clearly toward an execution response.]
[Call out the dependency, risk, or open question that could change the recommendation if additional diligence evidence points a different way.]
[Close with the reason this matters for the broader story, including the decision leadership should be prepared to make.]

slides.title: [Current vs target]
slides.strapline: [Use when the audience needs a richer side-by-side comparison with evidence and implications on both sides.]
leftBody: [Describe today's operating model with concrete evidence and explain the gap that is driving performance pressure or execution drag.]
[Explain the root cause in practical terms so the audience can see how process, ownership, or control design is shaping the issue.]
[State the business consequence in revenue, margin, timing, or decision quality rather than staying at a descriptive level.]
[Name the affected function, owner, or governance step that currently limits the business from improving the outcome.]
rightBody: [Describe the future-state design or management response and explain how it directly resolves the operating issue seen on the left-hand side.]
[Link the target state to an implementation action, accountable owner, and near-term milestone that leadership can actually sponsor.]
[State the expected benefit in measurable terms so the target state feels like a genuine management decision instead of a generic aspiration.]
[Call out the most important dependency or enabler required to move from the current model to the target model.]

slides.title: [Detailed evidence table]
slides.strapline: [Use for a table-led slide with four rich rows and explicit read-through.]

rows: Explain the gap, the management response, and the implication for operating discipline.
Link the shortfall to execution cadence, governance, or the need for clearer ownership.
State what the data says about the root cause and why it matters for the diligence conclusion.
Keep the read-through rich enough to be useful but short enough to preserve one-page readability.

insights: [Call out the most material gap and explain why it matters for execution certainty, management credibility, or transaction value.]
[Translate the evidence into an operating implication that leadership can act on in the next working session.]
[State the financial or control implication so the reader understands why the issue matters beyond local process performance.]
[Name the priority action and the evidence still needed to close the point with confidence.]
